name: rtpflood
title: rtpflood

commands.name: rtpflood
commands.usage: rtpflood sourcename destinationname srcport destport numpackets seqno timestamp SSID
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# rtpflood 192.168.1.202 192.168.1.1 5060 5061 1000 3 123456789 kali

Will flood port 5061 from port 5060 1000 times
Using sequence_number 3 timestamp 123456789 SSID 0

We have IP_HDRINCL

Number of Packets sent:

Sent 289 160 286

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=3bab20bee09230288c40bdb18a69f3e4e4b602e0, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Tool to flood any RTP device
A command line tool used to flood any device that is
processing RTP.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use rtpflood because ### rtpflood.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `rtpflood sourcename destinationname srcport destport numpackets seqno timestamp SSID` as documented upstream for rtpflood.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair rtpflood with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `rtpflood --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: rtpflood sourcename destinationname srcport destport numpackets seqno timestamp SSID

examples: rtpflood sourcename destinationname srcport destport numpackets seqno timestamp SSID
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### rtpflood.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install rtpflood
Install with: sudo apt install rtpflood
